title: Open Files
subtitle: Rooks need roads
summary: Use open files and the seventh rank to turn rooks into active attacking pieces.
attribution: Original prose; ideas after Nimzowitsch, My System. No book text reproduced.

steps.title: Give the rook a road
steps.markdown: A rook is strongest when it has an [[open-file|open file]]: a clear vertical road with no pawns in the way. Once a rook enters the [[seventh-rank|seventh rank]], it can start attacking pawns from the side and restrict the king near home.

steps.title: Invade the seventh
steps.markdown: The a-file is open. Move the rook to the seventh rank and win the first loose pawn.

hints.text: The pawn on a7 is loose.

feedback.correct: Yes. The rook reaches the seventh rank with tempo and starts attacking pawns from behind the enemy lines.
feedback.wrong: Look for the rook move that uses the open a-file.

steps.title: What changed?
steps.markdown: Why is the rook on a7 more active than it was on a1?

options.label: It starts attacking pawns from the side
options.explain: Right. The rook immediately attacks b7. If that pawn falls, the rest of the seventh rank becomes vulnerable.

options.label: It became a queen
options.explain: It is still a rook. The improvement is activity, not promotion.

options.label: It blocks its own pawns
options.explain: There are no white pawns on the a-file, so the rook has room to work.

steps.title: Activity over decoration
steps.markdown: Rooks do not belong in the corner forever. Find open files, occupy them, then look for entry squares on the seventh rank.
